I don't know 15 years ago. Yeah, okay. So a million a week 15 years ago real money, right? I Mean, this is a $10,000 sale at a distance Our sale was they were pretty good at our sale was a $10,000 sale, but that was the basic sale We had a lot of different things. We had monogramming where they had to buy monogram machines we had a I can't remember a lot of them, but there are a lot of different businesses that they had to buy other equipment for So our sales were anywhere from now if they had a computer We didn't make them take a computer, but most people didn't so they were it was a $10,000 package But it ended up being either a 9000 to a $14,000 package somewhere in that neighborhood All right, so you want to play come play He and I are yours for the duration of the evening you now know the story While they come up last question I have for you before they get in position Is if somebody pressed you about besides the fact that what you just said okay, they trusted you Implicitly what else would you attribute the success of this thing too if you had to narrow it to say two other things I sincerely care about people. I want them to do well and I'll do whatever I have to do to help him my mother trained me properly I had my father died when I was nine I Had a mother that taught me I Don't know just a lot of things that worked and and I followed them Don't know where I was going. Hi go good point When you had your idea What was that what was in your mind was the next process in terms of now? I've got this idea You've got the crazy computer programmer with the rat How did you now decide to go in terms of what media so where did I guess so I got like five questions in one? Once you had the idea What was the next step that you thought in terms of taking it out to the public? What was your thinking in terms of okay? I've got this idea and now I want to get it out there Thank you. I know your question. I can answer it. All right I developed My program or developed the four businesses. I put them together I Ran ads in Indiana and newspapers Like the other people were doing I said I went to see Because I was going to copy their programs of their style We were in the dialer automatic dialer business before that time We were the largest automatic dialer company in the United States and We're still doing automatic dialer when I'm doing putting this together. It still hasn't been outlawed yet It's going to be in another month or so, but it hadn't happened yet And so I Put the program together I Put ads in six towns in Indiana three small and three large and I put on six programs I have a motor home and I got my motor home and I went to the town I put the program on the next morning I Got up and started What doing the program and every night I would change it I would try it a little something different the next night the next night next night next night And by the sixth night I had sold six units at $8,500 each But I didn't think it was enough To do it the way that I had been doing it because my salesman usually sell half of what I do That's pretty normal and I have some that sells as good as I do but not very many and so I Wasn't really happy with what happened to me that week I had made a decent profit, but I hadn't made enough profit to do the things I wanted to do with it So I came in the door We were in the dialer business and we did just advertising And in the sales magazine, that's all and One of my sons met me at the door asked me how I did and I told him I did all right, but I'm not happy with it He said what are you doing that for why don't we just run ads like we do in the dialer business and and let's see what happens And I said okay, let's try it. So we ran two ads And I think specialty salesman and direct salesman magazine and we sold like I Think it was 14 sales the first week of Having that in and I said Andy that's it So those ads were lead generation ads right brought in holy lead generation there's all the harm They're basically calling to get the literature Can you talk about what you follow after you got the lead? What what was the next step? What did you send? Was it just a letter was it no no that whole big package? We sent them a package that was a big envelope It had full color literature in it It had a picture of my farm and had a picture of my family It had a picture of all the Program advisors it had all the offices in it. It had it added a picture of Kind of a picture of what we were doing trying to and then I Again in that packet had a three hour cassette tape that they had to listen to before that We wouldn't sell them unless they listen to that to that cassette Because I want you to know everything you possibly can know and then I want you to write down your questions That you have this is what the girl told them I want you to write down the questions You have when you call your program advisor I want you to ask him as many questions you possibly can ask him because that's what we pay him for So don't worry about questions get write them all down And then they just went then they call a program advisor and the program advisors Job and income was to sell And they worked on straight commission I didn't pay him any salary at all Thanks great stuff You actually just answered my question so thanks all right okay, what was your question? I already answered it. Okay, John
